title: culture's bot framework is now a thin forward to agentirc.bots — culture/bots/* and culture/cli/bot.py are re-export shims, the in-tree implementation is gone, and every culture bot verb plus the bot-coupled tests work unchanged against the agentirc-owned BotManager

targets.text: culture's bot framework is now a thin forward to agentirc.bots — culture/bots/* and culture/cli/bot.py are re-export shims, the in-tree implementation is gone, and every culture bot verb plus the bot-coupled tests work unchanged against the agentirc-owned BotManager
after the PR, culture/bots/* and culture/cli/bot.py contain no implementation logic — only re-exports/forwards — and git shows the implementation files deleted
culture maintainers + the mesh: anyone running culture bot verbs, the IRCd's bot/webhook lifecycle, and CI that exercises bot-coupled tests
operators running culture bot ... and the IRCd bot/webhook lifecycle observe identical behaviour to pre-cutover (CLI verbs, webhook dispatch, bot events)
culture/bots/* and culture/cli/bot.py are thin re-export shims to agentirc.bots.*; the in-tree bot implementation is deleted; agentirc-cli is pinned to a version that ships agentirc.bots

importing culture.bots.bot_manager / .bot / .config / .filter_dsl / .template_engine / .http_listener after the cutover yields the agentirc classes and passes existing tests with no AttributeError on any previously-public-or-test-reached symbol

culture hosts a full in-tree bot implementation under culture/bots/* (bot_manager, bot, config, filter_dsl, template_engine, http_listener, virtual_client, system/) and culture/cli/bot.py; agentirc 9.6.0 (currently pinned) has no agentirc.bots
agentirc-cli is bumped from the current >=9.6,<10 pin to one that guarantees agentirc.bots exists (>=9.7.0), and uv.lock is refreshed in the same PR

completes the copy-first->test->culture-replaces protocol (extraction never loses functionality), unblocks front-door slimming (#33), and culture inherits two agentirc bugfixes (bot-name path traversal, registry corruption on partial start) for free
the two agentirc bugfixes (bot-name path traversal via validate_bot_name, registry corruption on partial start) are present by virtue of forwarding, with no buggy logic re-ported into culture

not re-porting bot logic into culture, not changing the bot YAML spec or ~/.culture/bots on-disk layout, not touching other backends' harnesses, not a multi-PR rollout — single pin->forward->delete PR
the bot YAML spec and ~/.culture/bots on-disk layout (BOTS_DIR, BOT_CONFIG_FILE) are unchanged, and no other backend harness is touched

agentirc-cli pinned & lockfile refreshed; culture/bots/* + culture/cli/bot.py are shims with the implementation deleted; culture bot verbs still work; the four named bot-coupled tests + test_cli_bot + test_bot + test_events_bot_trigger stay green; no window where the bot framework exists in neither repo
after pin->forward->delete, /run-tests is green (the four named tests + test_cli_bot + test_bot + test_events_bot_trigger + test_cli_server bot cases) with no in-tree bot implementation remaining
culture bot create must preserve its three-part <server>-<owner>-<name> naming (or a deliberate decision to accept agentirc's two-part <owner>-<name>), since agentirc bot create has no server concept
culture bot create <owner> <name> still yields a bot whose nick matches the pre-cutover three-part form (or the user has explicitly accepted the two-part form), verified by test_cli_bot
the IRCd bot/webhook lifecycle must not double-start: agentirc IRCd.start() now drives BotManager.start(); if culture's server wrapper also starts a BotManager the listener binds twice
with agentirc 9.7+ IRCd.start() driving BotManager.start(), culture's _run_server starts exactly one BotManager and binds webhook_port exactly once (no double-start)

re-export shims must expose every symbol culture's own code AND tests import, including deep internals: EmitEventSpec, _check_rate, _DynamicEventType, _render_data_values, and the system-bot discovery hook (discover_system_bots)
culture.bots.system stays real culture code (agentirc ships no bots.system); culture's welcome system bot is still discovered and registered after cutover, verified by test_welcome_bot

culture/cli/server.py must be reworked (not just import-forwarded): let agentirc IRCd.start() own BotManager lifecycle and inject culture's system-bot discovery, instead of constructing+starting a second culture BotManager
_run_server no longer constructs a second BotManager; it injects sys.modules['agentirc.bots.system']=culture.bots.system before ircd.start(); test_welcome_bot passes and webhook_port binds once

tasks.summary: Pin agentirc-cli and refresh the lockfile
acceptance_criteria: uv.lock refreshed in the same change; 'uv run python -c "import agentirc.bots"' succeeds

tasks.summary: Forward the six core bot modules to agentirc.bots.* as re-export shims
acceptance_criteria: culture/bots/{bot_manager,bot,config,filter_dsl,template_engine,http_listener}.py each re-export their agentirc.bots counterpart and contain no implementation logic

importing culture.bots.bot_manager/.bot/.config/.filter_dsl/.template_engine/.http_listener yields the agentirc classes with no AttributeError on EmitEventSpec, _check_rate, _DynamicEventType, _render_data_values

the two agentirc bugfixes (validate_bot_name path-traversal guard, registry-corruption-on-partial-start) are present via forwarding with no buggy logic re-ported; test_bot and test_events_bot_trigger green

tasks.summary: Rework culture/cli/server.py: sys.modules system-bot bridge + single BotManager
acceptance_criteria: _run_server constructs no second BotManager (IRCd.start() owns lifecycle); webhook_port binds exactly once (no double-start)

tasks.summary: Forward culture/cli/bot.py to agentirc verbs, preserving 3-part naming
acceptance_criteria: culture bot create/start/stop/list/inspect/archive/unarchive forward to the agentirc bot verbs
culture bot create yields a nick of the form <server>-<owner>-<name>; test_cli_bot green

tasks.summary: Keep culture/bots/system as real code; welcome bot loads via the bridge
acceptance_criteria: culture.bots.system stays real culture code (not deleted/forwarded); its imports resolve against the config shim
the welcome system bot is discovered and registered through agentirc's BotManager.load_system_bots via the sys.modules bridge; test_welcome_bot green

tasks.summary: Delete the in-tree implementation; preserve YAML/on-disk contract
acceptance_criteria: git shows the in-tree bot implementation deleted; culture/bots/* and culture/cli/bot.py contain only re-exports/forwards
the bot YAML spec and ~/.culture/bots on-disk layout (BOTS_DIR, BOT_CONFIG_FILE) are unchanged; no other backend harness is touched

tasks.summary: Full verification: tests green + behaviour parity, no coverage gap window
acceptance_criteria: /run-tests green incl. test_bot_event_dispatch_span, test_bot_run_span, test_metrics_bots, test_welcome_bot, test_cli_bot, test_bot, test_events_bot_trigger, and test_cli_server bot cases

culture bot verbs + IRCd bot/webhook lifecycle behave identically to pre-cutover (CLI verbs, webhook dispatch, bot events); pin->forward->delete done in one PR with no window where the framework exists in neither repo
